question: Which phrase means 'to react to something as if it is much worse than it actually is'?
He _____ always forget his keys. (typical, characteristic behaviour — present)
Which word means 'to view someone with great disrespect'?
Read: 'Increasingly, parents are seeking a greater work-life balance.' What does 'work-life balance' refer to?
choices: working as many hours as possible
balancing time between professional work and personal life
choosing between a career and having children
using technology to work from home

question: I _____ live near the beach when I was young. (past state — no longer true)
Which adjective describes someone who 'has a very positive but perhaps unrealistic view of the world'?
The house _____ we just walked past belongs to my grandfather. (defining)
Which phrase means 'to harbour a deep sense of hatred towards someone'?
Read: 'At the end of the story, Monks gives Oliver the money that is owed to him and leaves the country. Oliver is adopted by Mr Brownlow.' What does the ending tell us about Mr Brownlow?
choices: that he is a cruel and selfish man
that he is a kind and generous man
that he is a dishonest man

question: Which relative pronoun is used for places?
Which word describes 'a person whose job is to look after a house'?
She _____ been constantly arriving late and didn't seem interested in her work.
Which linking word in an opinion essay signals a CONTRASTING idea?
Rewrite using a participle clause: 'Users who are used to the old system may find the new one confusing.'
choices: Users used to the old system may find the new one confusing.
Users using to the old system may find the new one confusing.
Users which used to the old system may find the new one confusing.
Users who using the old system may find the new one confusing.

question: Which phrase means 'to speak to someone angrily about something wrong they have done'?
She is _____ about the environment and thinks we should all help stop climate change.

Read: 'People sometimes think that stressful or upsetting events shape us negatively, and joyful events have a positive effect, but to my mind, the opposite can also be true.' Is this a fact or an opinion?
choices: a fact because it is based on research
an opinion because of the phrase 'to my mind'
an opinion because it mentions joyful events

question: What does 'gathered together' mean in the context of Oliver Twist?
Which adjective means 'showing great skill and ability'?
My best friend, _____ I was usually talking to, used to get fed up with me whispering.
He _____ always be the first one to arrive at school. (typical repeated past behaviour — action)
Which phrase means 'to behave so as to set a positive example for others'?
Read: 'Around 50% of children in Jordan can access the internet.' Is this a fact or an opinion?
choices: an opinion because it talks about children
a fact because it is a verifiable statistic
a fact because it mentions Jordan

question: The company _____ I work has very strict rules.
Which phrase means 'to have a deep disagreement with a sibling'?
choices: a row with your best friend
a clash between you and a sibling
a newborn baby in the family

question: He's quite charming. This means he:
choices: has a very attractive and pleasant personality

question: In 'This is a bit of a sweeping statement, but younger people often take offence easily', this phrase is used to:
choices: acknowledge that the speaker may be generalising
introduce the conclusion of an essay

question: What does 'beg' mean in the context of Oliver Twist?
choices: to ask strangers for money or food

question: Rewrite: 'Sami lives round the corner. I play football with him.' (Use relative clause)
choices: Sami, which I play football with, lives round the corner.
Sami, who I play football with, lives round the corner.
Sami, whose I play football with, lives round the corner.
Sami, where I play football with, lives round the corner.

question: Read: 'Oliver was sad to leave the other orphans.' An orphan is:
choices: a person whose job is to look after children
a child whose parents have died
a child who runs away from home
a person who works in a workhouse

question: She _____ always the one who made people laugh. (past habit — now changed)
Which adjective describes someone who is 'very good at a task and highly skilled'?
Nasser says he's worried about climate change, but he drives everywhere and goes on holiday by plane twice a year. He's _____.
Read the opinion essay. The phrase 'I would argue that families generally remain as close as they ever were' is an example of:
In which sentence can you omit the relative pronoun without affecting the meaning?
choices: The house which was built in 1800 is historic.
My grandfather, who I admire greatly, is 90.
This is the app that you can keep in your pocket.
Amman, which is the capital, is beautiful.

question: What does 'make ends meet' mean?
choices: to earn a great deal of money
to have just enough money to buy what you need

question: She _____ forever losing her phone when she was a teenager.
Which phrase means 'a person who is a model of excellent behaviour'?
The man, _____ won the prize, is my neighbour.
Which phrase means 'to admire someone so much that you treat them as if they are perfect and can do no wrong'?
